quick_facts.label: Employment level
quick_facts.value: In the 2025 ISRO ICRB Scientist/Engineer ‘SC’ advertisements, the post was Level 10 of the Central Pay Matrix and a Group A Gazetted post at constituent ISRO centres.

quick_facts.label: Core eligibility pattern
quick_facts.value: The 2025 Electronics, Mechanical and Computer Science campaigns required a relevant BE/BTech or equivalent with at least 65% aggregate marks or CGPA 6.84/10; accepted disciplines and conditions are campaign-specific and must be checked in the applicable advertisement.

quick_facts.label: Recruitment routes
quick_facts.value: Recent 2025 ICRB recruitment used both a GATE-score route and a written-test route for Scientist/Engineer ‘SC’ vacancies; these are separate campaigns, not permanent universal rules for every discipline or year.

quick_facts.label: Starting-pay context
quick_facts.value: In the 2025 advertisements, the minimum basic pay stated for Level 10 was ₹56,100 per month, with DA, HRA and transport allowance under extant rules. This is dated compensation context; allowances, pension coverage and take-home pay vary with applicable government rules and posting.

quick_facts.label: Posting mobility
quick_facts.value: Appointees may be posted to any ISRO/Department of Space centre or unit and may be transferred on a need basis. Recent campaigns listed locations including Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Ahmedabad, Sriharikota, Thiruvananthapuram, Mahendragiri, Valiamala and Hassan, depending on discipline and vacancy.

sections.heading: What the role contributes to
paragraphs: Scientist/Engineer ‘SC’ is an entry scientific-and-technical officer role within ISRO/Department of Space centres and units. ISRO describes those organisations as undertaking research and development in space applications, space science and technology, including developing the capacity to design, build and launch launch vehicles and communication and remote-sensing satellites.

The precise day-to-day assignment is not fixed by the ‘SC’ title alone: it depends on the recruiting discipline, centre, programme and posting. Applicants should therefore read the post code, eligible discipline, tentative centre/unit and vacancy details in the specific ICRB advertisement rather than assume a single generic job description.
bullets: Potential technical context spans space applications, space science, launch-vehicle work, satellite systems and associated engineering or computing functions.
The 2025 ICRB EMC recruitment was specifically organised around Electronics, Mechanical and Computer Science post codes; other ICRB campaigns have advertised different discipline groups.

sections.heading: Entry routes through ISRO ICRB
paragraphs: ICRB has used more than one selection design. In the April 2025 EMC campaign, candidates with the required degree and a valid discipline-specific GATE score were shortlisted for interview on GATE score, not GATE marks or rank. The stated final-panel weighting was 50% GATE score and 50% interview marks.

In the May 2025 EMC campaign, ISRO used a written test followed by interview. The written paper was an objective 120-minute test with a discipline-specific Part A and an aptitude/ability Part B. Candidates were shortlisted for interview from written-test performance, and final panels gave 50% weight to the written test and 50% to interview.

Eligibility, disciplines, number of posts, age cut-off, reservation conditions, fee arrangements, test pattern and deadlines change by advertisement. For example, the 2025 EMC written-test advertisement set the general age limit at 28 years as of June 16, 2025 and accepted online applications only during its stated application window; neither date should be treated as a standing rule.
bullets: Degree baseline in the cited 2025 EMC campaigns: relevant BE/BTech or equivalent, normally 65% aggregate or CGPA 6.84/10.
For the cited GATE route, valid EC, ME or CS GATE scores corresponded to the Electronics, Mechanical and Computer Science posts respectively.
For the cited written-test route, Part A had 80 discipline MCQs with negative marking, while Part B had up to 15 aptitude/ability MCQs for 20 marks without negative marking.
Use ISRO’s Careers and Current Opportunities pages to identify the currently applicable ICRB notice, then rely on that notice and its FAQs.

sections.heading: Skills signalled by the selection process
paragraphs: The documented interview rubrics indicate the capabilities ISRO assessed in the 2025 campaigns: technical/academic knowledge, technical awareness relevant to the applicant’s specialisation, presentation and communication, comprehension, and academic achievement. This makes strong discipline fundamentals necessary but not sufficient; candidates also need to explain technical ideas clearly and demonstrate awareness of their engineering specialisation.

For written-test campaigns, preparation should additionally cover timed, objective discipline questions and general aptitude/ability. The exact coverage, scoring and qualifying standards must be taken from the current advertisement because they can change.
bullets: Technical depth in the eligible engineering discipline.
Awareness of developments relevant to the declared technical specialisation.
Comprehension and performance under an objective, timed examination format when a written-test route is used.

sections.heading: Centres, units and mobility
paragraphs: The workplace is an ISRO/Department of Space centre or unit, rather than a role limited to ICRB’s Bengaluru recruitment administration. In the cited 2025 GATE recruitment, indicative postings varied by branch: for example, Electronics vacancies named HSFC/URSC Bengaluru, NRSC Hyderabad, SAC Ahmedabad, SDSC SHAR Sriharikota and VSSC Thiruvananthapuram; Mechanical and Computer Science lists differed.

The cited 2025 written-test advertisement states that appointees are liable to be posted at any ISRO/Department of Space centre or unit and may be transferred at any time on a need basis. Geographic preferences are therefore not guaranteed by applying under a particular post code.
bullets: Work may be located at research, spacecraft/satellite, launch-vehicle, propulsion, applications, tracking or other ISRO-centre environments, according to the employing unit and discipline.
Plan for all-India posting and transfer liability rather than treating the recruitment location as the assured work location.

sections.heading: Career progression
paragraphs: ISRO’s 2025 Scientist/Engineer ‘SC’ GATE-based advertisement describes a Merit Promotion Scheme for scientific and technical posts. Under the description in that notice, promotion to higher levels is delinked from the availability of vacancies, and meritorious employees can progress through periodic merit-based performance review.

This is a framework for career advancement, not a promise of automatic promotion on a fixed timetable. Actual progression depends on the applicable scheme, performance assessment and service rules in force.
bullets: Scientific and technical progression is described as merit- and performance-review based.
Applicants should use ISRO’s Careers portal and the terms of their eventual appointment for the current progression provisions.

sections.heading: Compensation and benefits context
paragraphs: As stated in the 2025 ICRB advertisements, appointment as Scientist/Engineer ‘SC’ was at Pay Matrix Level 10 with a minimum basic pay of ₹56,100 per month. The notices also named DA, HRA and transport allowance under extant rules, making the actual monthly gross and take-home pay dependent on the prevailing allowance rates, city classification, deductions and accommodation situation.

The same notices described pension coverage as the New Pension Scheme/Unified Pension Scheme and listed benefits admissible under Central Government orders, including medical facilities for self and dependants, subsidised canteen, limited quarters in lieu of HRA, leave travel concession, group insurance and house-building advance. These are not a guaranteed individual package: availability and rules can change.
bullets: Use ₹56,100 per month only as the cited 2025 minimum basic-pay reference, not as a current estimate of total compensation.
Check the appointment offer, current Central Government allowance orders and the posting centre’s housing arrangements for applicable benefits.
